data.title: January saw a surprising 130,000 job increase in the US, beating expectations. Unemployment rate dropped to 4.3%. However, sweeping revisions erased 898,000 jobs from estimates in 2024-2025, showing the weakest non-recession job growth since 2003. Wage growth also accelerated. Former Fed economist questions if January's report masks a weaker trend. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: The US added 130,000 jobs in January, surpassing expectations of 70,000, with a drop in unemployment rate from 4.4% to 4.3%. Wage growth increased to 0.4% monthly and 3.7% annually. However, historical revisions reduced 898,000 jobs from payroll estimates between April 2024 and March 2025, with total employment growth in 2025 revised from 584,000 to 181,000. The average monthly job growth in 2024 was only 15,000, the lowest outside a recession since 2003. This has led to a debate among economists about whether January's data signals stable growth or conceals a weaker trend. Former Fed economist Claudia Sahm highlighted the substantial downward revisions, calling the January report a mix of good news and significant revisions.

data.title: NexGold grants 671,742 RSUs and 348,607 DSUs to officers and directors, vesting in 3 tranches, to align leadership interests.
data.description: In Toronto, on February 9, 2026, NexGold Mining Corp. (TSXV: NEXG; OTCQX: NXGCF) disclosed that its Board of Directors approved the issuance of 671,742 Restricted Share Units (RSUs) and 348,607 Deferred Share Units (DSUs) to executives and non-executive board members under the 2024 Omnibus Equity Incentive Plan, in compliance with TSX Venture Exchange regulations. The RSUs and DSUs will vest in three equal portions commencing one year from the date of grant, with each unit converting to one common share of the company upon settlement as per the Plan. The objective of the grants is to align the interests of leadership with those of shareholders, acknowledge contributions, and promote long-term retention and performance.

data.title: Core CPI inflation rate at its lowest since early 2021, likely affecting Fed's decisions. S&P 500 stabilizes.
data.description: The core Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation rate has decreased to the lowest point seen since early 2021. This data suggests a potential shift in the Federal Reserve's approach, as the post CPI inflation information indicates a need for caution. This development has also led to a stabilization in the S&P 500 index.

data.title: Jobs report shows strong hiring with 130k payrolls, 172k private-sector jobs, and 4.3% unemployment rate; S&P 500 fluctuates.
data.description: Employment numbers show a rise of 130,000 payrolls, including 172,000 private-sector jobs. The unemployment rate decreases to 4.3%. Analysts suggest that despite the strong hiring, the actual numbers may be exaggerated. This news caused fluctuations in the S&P 500 index, according to reports from Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Former Pfizer Exec Geno J. Germano appointed as Chairman of Entera Bio's Board, bringing global leadership experience. Multiple oral peptide programs advancing toward key milestones in 2026.
data.description: Entera has welcomed a new chairman to its board who brings extensive senior global leadership experience from major biopharma companies like Pfizer and Wyeth. The addition comes as Entera progresses various oral peptide programs towards important milestones in 2026. The new chairman's background in the industry indicates a promising direction for Entera as they work towards achieving success in their upcoming endeavors.

data.title: CRISPR Therapeutics' gene therapy Casgevy garnered $54 million in Q4 2025, with a total of 64 patients receiving infusions. A total of 147 patients initiated treatment through the first cell collection during the year, nearly tripling patient initiations compared to 2024. Casgevy has reimbursement covering roughly 90% of eligible patients in the U.S. and is also reimbursed across multiple European and Middle Eastern markets.
data.description: CRISPR Therapeutics AG reported its financial results for Q4 and full year 2025, with Casgevy, its gene-editing therapy for sickle cell disease and beta thalassemia, generating $54 million in Q4 and $116 million for the year. 64 patients received infusions in 2025, with 147 patients initiating treatment in the first cell collection. Patient initiations nearly tripled from 2024, indicating momentum for 2026. William Blair expects higher volumes to lead to increased revenue in 2026. Reimbursement now covers approximately 90% of eligible U.S. patients, with coverage in various European and Middle Eastern markets. Partner Vertex Pharmaceuticals Inc. was mentioned in the report.

data.title: Stock market surges as Dow Jones climbs after CPI data, with small caps leading; Crypto name soars.
data.description: Today in the stock market, the Dow Jones index saw an increase following a report of lower-than-anticipated Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation numbers. Small-cap stocks led the way in gains. The market also showed strength in the S&P 500 index. Additionally, a notable cryptocurrency experienced a significant surge in value.

data.title: Chronicle Studios and The Hive Studio partner for two animated series on YouTube channel.
data.description: In a joint announcement today, AI media technology company Chronicle Studios and Academy Award-nominated animation production company The Hive Studio have partnered on two animated series for their YouTube channel. The goth musical comedy "The Vampair" created by Daria Cohen of "Hazbin Hotel" fame, and the teen comedy "The Normal MFer" by CC0 Studios, Joe McNaney, and Alex Orrelle of "Space Jam: A New Legacy" will be featured. This collaboration brings together creative talents from San Francisco and Tel Aviv, Israel, marking an exciting venture for both companies.

data.title: Logansport Financial Corp. declares $.45 quarterly dividend per share for Q1 2026; payable on April 13.
data.description: Logansport Financial Corp., the holding company for Logansport Savings Bank in Indiana, has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.45 per share of its common stock for Q1 2026. The dividend will be paid on April 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 13, 2026.

data.title: Rivian's stock rises post Q4 beat, reporting a $1.28B revenue & an adjusted loss of 54 cents - exceeding expectations.
data.description: Rivian Automotive, Inc. (Nasdaq: RIVN) saw a rise in its stock trading following the announcement of better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results. The company reported an adjusted loss of 54 cents per share, outperforming the expected 68-cent loss. Revenue stood at $1.28 billion, exceeding the forecasted $1.27 billion. Benchmark analyst Mickey Legg maintained a Buy rating on the stock and increased the price forecast from $18 to $25, citing the company's significant progress in the latest quarter. Rivian's return to positive gross profit, aided by Software & Services growth, helped mitigate fluctuations in the core auto business before the R2 launch. Despite ongoing challenges in automotive margins, positive gross profit for the entire year is attributed to enhanced cost control and execution. This article can be read in full on Benzinga.com.
